text.title: Cloud Waste Elimination and Non-Production Scheduling Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Cleanup script deletes resources on first run; legitimate volumes lost; FinOps trust collapses.
antipattern.root-cause: Dry-run not enforced as default mode.
antipattern.fix: Make `--apply` mandatory for mutation; default mode prints preview to stdout.

antipattern.symptom: Initial sweep finds $30k/month waste; six months later, same waste re-accumulated.
antipattern.fix: Schedule a weekly Lambda + alert; require human approval only for prod.

antipattern.symptom: 20% of resources carry no waste-exception tag yet survive every sweep — 'someone said leave them'.
antipattern.fix: Reject untagged exemptions at scanner level; require tag with reason+owner+expiry.

antipattern.symptom: Staging $40k/month, used 45h/week out of 168 — 73% waste rate.
antipattern.fix: Apply Instance Scheduler or equivalent; target 70% non-prod savings.
